expressions.english: reading material; something to read
to read a book
to read a newspaper
to read loudly; to read out loud
expressions.notes:
크다 = to be big; to be tall; to be loud
큰 is the adjective form of 크다.
소리 = sound

expressions.english: Have you read this book?
expressions.notes:
이 = this
-어/여 봤다 = to try + V-ing

expressions.english: Did you read that article?
Is there anything to read?
expressions.notes:
뭔가 = something (subject)
뭔가 is the contracted form of 무언가.
읽을거리 = reading material, something to read

expressions.english: Read out loud, please.
Can I read this magazine?
I don't know how to read Chinese characters.
expressions.notes:
한자 = Korean name for Chinese characters
-(으)ㄹ 줄 모르다 = to not know how to + V

expressions.english: I feel sleepy when I read a book.
expressions.notes: 졸이 오다 = to be sleepy; to feel drowsy

expressions.english: Have you finished reading the book I lent you?
expressions.notes:
빌려주다 = to lend
다 + verb = to finish + V-ing

expressions.korean: 주말 동안에 이 책을 다 읽었어요.
expressions.english: I finished this book over the weekend.
expressions.notes:
주말 = weekend
동안 = during, while; for + period of time

expressions.korean: 그 책 다 읽으면 저 좀 빌려주세요.
expressions.english: When you finish the book, please lend it to me.

expressions.korean: 이 잡지는 재미가 없어서 읽기 싫어요.
expressions.english: This magazine is not interesting, so I don't want to read it.
expressions.notes:
잡지 = magazine
재미가 없다 = to be not fun; to be not funny; to be not interesting; to be boring
-기 싫다 = to not feel like + V-ing; to not want to + V

expressions.korean: 어제 책을 읽느라고 밤에 늦게 잤어요.
expressions.english: I went to bed late last night because I was reading a book.
expressions.notes:
-느라고 = because + S + was V-ing
늦다 = to be late
늦게 is the adverb form of 늦다.
자다 = to sleep

expressions.korean: 한글을 읽을 줄은 아는데 쓸 줄은 몰라요.
expressions.english: I know how to read Hangeul, but I don't know how to write it.
expressions.notes:
한글 = Hangeul (Korean alphabet)
-(으)ㄹ 줄 알다 = to know how to + V
쓰다 = to write

expressions.korean: 과제 때문에 읽어야 할 책을 아직 안 읽었어요.
expressions.english: Reading that book is my assignment, but I haven't read it yet.
expressions.notes:
과제 = homework; assignment
때문에 = because (of), since
아직 = (not) yet

expressions.korean: 이 책은 어린이들이 읽기에는 어려운 것 같아요.
expressions.english: I think this book is difficult for children to read.
expressions.notes:
어린이 = kid, child
-기에 = (to be too + adjective) + to + V
어렵다 = to be difficult
어려운 is the adjective form of 어렵다.
- 것 같다 = it seems; looks like + S + V

expressions.korean: 그 책 너무 재미있어서 하루만에 다 읽었어요.
expressions.english: The book is so interesting that I finished reading it in only one day.
